name: Remote System Discovery
description: Remote system discovery using a system utility like `ping`, `nslookup`, or `net.exe` is detected. Adversaries may use these utilities to discover systems on the network. This detection is triggered when an unusual number of discovery commands are executed from a single host, which could indicate a reconnaissance phase of an attack. It's often followed by lateral movement or other malicious activities. This prompt helps investigate the source of the discovery activity and determine if it's a legitimate administrative task or a malicious reconnaissance.

prompts.prompt: Investigate the remote system discovery activity from host `{hostname}` at `{timestamp}`. The command line detected was `{command_line}` executed by the user `{user_name}`. Determine if this is a legitimate administrative action or a malicious reconnaissance attempt. Provide a summary of your findings, including the number of hosts scanned and the outcome of the discovery. Recommend immediate containment and remediation steps.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname of the system where the discovery activity was detected.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line of the process (e.g., ping, nslookup, net.exe).

parameters.name: user_name
parameters.description: The user account under which the process was executed.
